interventions.description: Flagged E2E math borderline for standalone recurrence kernel (0.98% deflated, below 1% threshold)

citation.claim: Monitor warned that standalone GDN recurrence kernel at 1.53x speedup only yields 0.98% deflated E2E, below the 1% threshold
citation.quoted_content:
Champion's own analysis: '797 GB/s gives 1.53x speedup -> deflated E2E may be below threshold'
Verified: 5.67% x (1-1/1.53) / 2 = 0.98% < 1.0% threshold

interventions.description: Definitively showed standalone recurrence is sub-threshold per roofline analysis (0.88% deflated at 92% BW)

citation.claim: Roofline analysis proved standalone kernel is definitively sub-threshold: even at 92% BW (795 GB/s), only 0.88% deflated E2E
citation.quoted_content:
At 92% BW (795 GB/s): 1.45x speedup -> 0.88% deflated E2E < 1.0% threshold
Even at theoretical peak (1.57x): deflated = 1.03% -- physically unachievable
STANDALONE KERNEL IS DEFINITIVELY SUB-THRESHOLD

interventions.description: Flagged f_decode overestimate (6.96% used vs correct 6.34%)

citation.claim: Monitor corrected champion's f_decode from 6.96% to 6.34% because z-norm is outside the custom op
citation.quoted_content:
z-norm (triton_per_fused_1) is OUTSIDE custom op -- cannot be fused
Correct fuseable f_decode = 6.34% (not 6.96% as champion's roofline script used)
At 85% BW: deflated E2E = 1.01% (barely above threshold)

interventions.description: Flagged gap between Tier 2 evidence (1.11x) and proposal claim (1.53x)

citation.claim: Tier 2 benchmark showed only 1.11x speedup vs champion's 1.53x claim in proposal
citation.quoted_content:
Sent WARNING about Tier 2 evidence gap (1.11x Triton vs 1.53x claimed)
Sent CRITICAL about ncu BW-occupancy diminishing returns (85% BW unachievable)

interventions.description: ncu BW-occupancy diminishing returns show 85% BW claim is unachievable

citation.claim: ncu evidence shows 3x occupancy increase yields only 11% BW improvement; 85% BW is not achievable
citation.quoted_content:
ncu evidence shows BW-occupancy relationship has diminishing returns on L40S
Going from 42% to ~100% occupancy will yield at most +8-10% more BW -> ceiling ~615 GB/s
At 70% BW with CUDA C++: speedup ~ 1.14-1.17x, E2E deflated ~ 0.44% <- FAR BELOW 1%

interventions.description: Flagged bandwidth math error (champion computed >864 GB/s, exceeding L40S peak) and in_proj_ba torch.compile risk

citation.claim: Champion-2 computed 1055 GB/s exceeding L40S theoretical peak of 864 GB/s, and in_proj_ba pivot carries torch.compile graph-change risk like Round 1 op008 failure
citation.quoted_content:
BANDWIDTH MATH ERROR: Champion computed 1055 GB/s > 864 GB/s (theoretical peak) -- resolved it unsupported
PIVOT TO in_proj_ba GEMM: Champion considering fusing in_proj_ba into custom op. This conflicts with orchestr

interventions.description: Flagged that champion must check existing op013/op017 fused GDN kernel in git before writing duplicate proposal

citation.quoted_content:
CRITICAL: Delegate found EXISTING fused GDN decode kernel in git history:
op013: 719f8b8e1 feat(op013): fused GDN decode kernel -- eliminates 96 CUDA graph nodes/step
op017: b96b095f5 feat(op017): BF16 state compression for fused GDN decode kernel
Champion action: Said 'Delegates still running. Proceed to proposal now.' -- WRONG

interventions.description: Proactively warned about warm-cache Triton speedup being misleading without cold-cache Triton data

citation.claim: Monitor sent proactive warning about warm-cache inflation before champion received Triton micro-experiment results showing impossible BW numbers (2362 GB/s vs L40S 864 GB/s peak)
citation.quoted_content:
CRITICAL GAP: NO cold-cache or pipeline measurement for Triton variants
BW numbers 1994-2362 GB/s impossible for DRAM (L40S max 864 GB/s) -> all L2 hits
FLAG SENT: [WARNING] to champion-3 about warm-cache Triton speedup being misleading

interventions.description: Flagged inflated speedup estimate from claiming launch overhead under CUDA graphs (near-zero in reality)

citation.claim: Champion claimed 4-6 us savings from kernel launch overhead under CUDA graphs, but CUDA graph replay has near-zero launch overhead; realistic savings only 3.2 us from fusion
citation.quoted_content:
Under CUDA graphs, kernel LAUNCH overhead ~ 0. The actual savings from eliminating conv1d + gating is their execution time ONLY IF fused kernel handles them at zero additional cost
Realistic total: ~5-9us savings -> speedup 1.14-1.26x
At 1.14-1.26x on 6.35% f_decode: E2E improvement = ~0.55-0.77% deflated -> LIKELY BELOW 1% GATE

interventions.description: Caught champion about to claim 1.12x production speedup via unsupported warm/cold interpolation when cold data showed 0.97x regression

citation.claim: BV=8 pipeline data was 0.97x SLOWER (negative), yet champion was interpolating to 1.12x production claim; monitor flagged as unsupported
citation.quoted_content:
BV=8 is SLOWER in cold-cache pipeline. The warm-cache speedup is entirely L2-dependent and does NOT transfer to production-like conditions.
Champion's plan: Update proposal with 'honest results' -- but in thinking, they plan to claim '1.12x production speedup via interpolation'

interventions.description: Flagged micro-experiment vs validator cold-cache discrepancy (debate ~2x vs actual ~1.35x)

citation.claim: Gate 5.2 revealed massive discrepancy: gate_up_proj 1.337x vs micro-experiment 2.981x, down_proj 0.99x vs 2.144x
citation.quoted_content:
gate_up_proj cold-cache: 1.337x (vs micro-exp ~2.981x claimed in debate)
down_proj cold-cache: 0.99x (vs micro-exp 2.144x claimed) -- barely above regression
Root cause identified: 40 Triton tiles vs CUTLASS 160 CTAs (4x SM utilization gap)

interventions.description: E2E improvement (1.06%) is likely system drift, not FP8 speedup -- same-day baseline at 7.742s matches opt 7.743s

citation.claim: Same-day baseline 7.742s essentially identical to opt 7.743s, suggesting 0% real improvement; 1.06% claim relies on cross-session Stage 1 baseline (7.825s)
citation.quoted_content:
CRITICAL FINDING: same-day baseline = 7.742s (essentially same as opt 7.743s)
The 1.06% improvement may be SYSTEM DRIFT not FP8 benefit
Opt vs same-day baseline: 7.743/7.742 = 1.00013x = 0% improvement
Stage 1's 7.825s is 1.07% slower than same-day baseline

interventions.description: Repeat --overwrite failure cycle destroying baseline and golden refs data twice

First sweep with --overwrite deleted Stage 1 baseline_bs8.json (json/ directory emptied)
Champion captured golden refs -> then ran opt sweep with --overwrite AGAIN -> deleted golden_refs.json
I sent WARNING at line 382 about repeat failure cycle.

| 13+ | up to 1.0 BF16 ULP | cuBLAS N=64 vs N=12416 tiling difference |

citation.claim: Campaign exhausted after 5 rounds with 5.1% cumulative lossless E2E improvement

anti_patterns.description: FP8 MLP debate projected 7.24% deflated E2E with floor of 2.40%; actual E2E was 1.06% (below even floor). Translation factor was 0.112x, far below empirical 0.33-0.5x range. FP8 weights partially fitting in L2 during isolated round-robin benchmarks created artificial cold-cache speedups that collapsed in production pipeline.

citation.claim: Debate projected 7.24% deflated E2E for op007 with floor of 2.40%, but actual was 1.06%
citation.quoted_content: **E2E projection**: 7.24% deflated (expected), 5.48% (worst, gate_up=1.5x), 2.40% (floor, down_proj only)

anti_patterns.description: Bottleneck analysis presented 64-92% BW utilization range as uniformly 'healthy' with 'no red flags', obscuring that down_proj at 64.5% had 35.5% headroom (nearly 3x gate_up_proj's 7.8%). This framing bias propagated through debate as the 'lossless MLP wall' argument, delaying discovery of the NN-layout optimization by 2 rounds.

citation.claim: Bottleneck analysis verdict of 'PASS - No red flags' lumped 64.5% BW (down_proj) with 92.2% BW (gate_up_proj), concealing optimization headroom
citation.quoted_content:
**PASS -- No red flags detected.** All three top decode bottleneck kernels exhibit
healthy baseline behavior:
- DRAM BW utilization is 64-92% of peak, consistent with well-tuned CUTLASS GEMM

anti_patterns.description: gate_up_proj at 37% f_decode was the single largest bottleneck but at 92.2% BW was essentially at the hardware ceiling. All 5 rounds correctly avoided it for BW optimization, but this dominant component effectively constrained the entire campaign's ceiling to ~1.30x via Amdahl's Law.

citation.claim: gate_up_proj at 37% f_decode and 92.2% BW utilization is essentially at the hardware ceiling with only 1.08x per-kernel ceiling
citation.quoted_content: **gate_up_proj (37% f_decode)** -- the largest bottleneck -- operates at **92.2% of peak DRAM bandwidth** (ncu-confirmed). The theoretical ceiling from BW optimization is only 1.08x, yielding ~3% E2E.

impact_classifications.counterfactual: Without this intervention, champion-1 may have spent GPU time implementing a standalone recurrence kernel that was provably sub-threshold, wasting a round's implementation budget
Without ncu evidence challenging the 85% BW claim, the op009 proposal would have carried an unrealistic E2E projection into implementation, increasing the gap between projected and actual results
Without this intervention, champion-3's proposal would have contained an unsupported 1.12x production claim based on interpolating a negative cold-cache data point, misleading the debate judge
Without the op013 prior art flag, champion-2 might have written a duplicate proposal without acknowledging existing feasibility evidence; however, champion would likely have found it independently

Without this system drift detection, op007 would have shipped with a 1.06% PASS verdict based on cross-session comparison that the campaign's own R1 lessons had invalidated. The escalation led to R2 re-evaluation at n=200 which correctly failed the track.

Without this warning about max-autotune not representing production, the investigation report would have incorrectly concluded Triton is bit-exact (only true under max-autotune). Champion ran production-path test and confirmed production diverges, closing the investigation gap.
Champion-3 independently identified the warm-cache inflation before reading the monitor's warning. The warning was proactively sent but champion had already self-corrected.
Without f_decode correction (6.96% to 6.34%), champion's roofline analysis would have overestimated E2E by ~10%, potentially making a sub-threshold optimization appear viable

changelog_entries.entry: Run 8 (Qwen3.5-4B/L40S): 5 coaching instances where monitors used actors' own profiling data to suggest fix paths. Most impactful: monitor used champion-1's roofline output to prove standalone recurrence sub-threshold (redirected to fused approach); monitor used champion-3's negative cold-cache data (0.97x) to prevent unsupported 1.12x interpolation claim. All 5 instances resulted in champion corrections within minutes.

Run 8 discovered recurring cuBLAS tile decomposition divergence pattern: 3 independent tracks (op008, op010-BN64-variant, op012) demonstrated that changing GEMM N-dimension triggers different CUTLASS tile selection during prefill M>=13, causing FP32 accumulation order changes that propagate through GDN recurrence to produce measurable accuracy loss. Root cause confirmed via systematic investigation (op008-investigate) with 17 tests covering eager, static compile, dynamic compile, CUDA graphs, M-sweep, N-sweep, and position sweep.

Run 8 quantified the cold-to-production translation gap: op007 FP8 showed 0.112x translation factor (1.06% measured vs 9.45% projected), far below expected 0.33-0.5x. Root cause: FP8 weights (22-45 MB) partially fit in L2 during isolated benchmarks while BF16 baseline (45-90 MB) does not, creating artificial cold-cache speedups. Recommendation: apply mandatory 0.33-0.5x translation factor to all Amdahl projections.

Run 8 causal analysis identified framing bias in bottleneck mining: presenting 64-92% BW range as uniformly 'healthy' obscured that down_proj (64.5% BW) had 35.5% headroom. This propagated through debate as the 'lossless MLP wall' argument, delaying NN-layout discovery by 2 rounds. Fix: per-kernel BW verdicts with <85% flagged as INVESTIGATE.

Run 8 transcript quality penalized for 5 wasted retries: op007 dispatch bugs (2 torch.compile fixes), op007 wrong correctness mode (exact_greedy instead of topk_relaxed), op008 --overwrite data destruction x2, op011 stale cache + dead conditional. Each retry consumed implementation time without advancing the campaign.

Run 8 identified 2 hallucinated data instances: (1) FP8 cold-cache speedup inflation (2.144x claimed vs 0.990x measured for down_proj), (2) cross-session baseline Gate 5.3b PASS claim (0% actual vs 1.06% claimed, invalidated by campaign's own R1 lessons about system drift).

nn_layout_surprise.description: op011 NN-layout optimization exceeded its Amdahl projection by 3x (3.95% measured vs 1.30% projected). The Amdahl deflation was overly conservative because the kernel is also active during prefill (M>16), contributing additional savings not captured in the decode-only model. This is the campaign's highest-impact single optimization.

citation.claim: op011 exceeded projection by 3x: 3.95% measured vs 1.30% deflated projection
citation.quoted_content: The original projection was 1.30% deflated E2E. The measured 3.95% significantly exceeds this -- the Amdahl deflation was overly conservative for this optimization.

technology_exhaustion.description: Campaign explored all available technology classes (Triton GEMM, CUDA C++, CUTLASS, FP8, NN layout, split-K) and concluded the optimization landscape is mechanically exhausted for this configuration. Two independent R5 debate champions confirmed this.

citation.claim: Campaign landscape confirmed exhausted by 2 independent R5 debate champions
citation.quoted_content: "Campaign landscape confirmed exhausted by 2 independent R5 debate champions: gate_up_proj at 92.2% BW ceiling, GDN recurrence at DRAM BW wall, all other components shipped or sub-threshold."

debate_convergence.description: In Round 1, all 3 champions independently proposed GDN qkvz+ba fusion (op008 candidate), demonstrating convergence of the champion team on the most promising optimization target. This 3/3 convergence was unprecedented in the campaign.

citation.claim: All 3 champions independently proposed GDN merge, demonstrating strong convergence
citation.quoted_content: **Champion**: champion-2 (primary), champion-3 (corroborating). All 3 champions independently proposed this.

torch_compile_lesson.description: Op011 discovered that shape-dependent conditionals (3<=M<=16) are dead code during torch.compile tracing -- the conditional is always evaluated to False at compilation time. This caused the first E2E sweep to show 0% improvement (kernel not dispatched). Fix: use environment variables for kernel selection, not tensor-shape conditionals.

citation.claim: Shape-dependent conditional was dead code during torch.compile tracing; fix was to remove shape dependency entirely
citation.quoted_content: Zero speedup with matched baseline | CRITICAL | Root-caused & fixed | Stale torch.compile cache was loading pre-optimization compiled graph. Cleared cache. Also fixed: shape-dependent conditional (`3<=M<=16`) was always False during torch.compile tracing -- removed shape dependency.

micro_experiment_lie.description: Op009's micro-experiment measured 27.80 us/layer but production pipeline showed 47.06 us -- a 1.7x discrepancy caused by L2 write-back overhead on 32 MB GDN state. This is the clearest example in the campaign of micro-experiments misrepresenting production performance.

citation.claim: Micro-experiment reported 30.87 us but production pipeline measured 46.42 us -- 1.5x discrepancy from L2 write-back overhead
citation.quoted_content:
My pipeline benchmark in the **same** setup measures:
- Triton BV=32 nw=1: **46.42 us** (361 GB/s)
The 30.87 us vs 46.42 us discrepancy (1.5x) is due to **L2 write-back overhead** in the cold-cache pipeline.

causal_dag_anomalies.description: Causal DAG identified 2 anomalies: (1) QUALITY_DROP in bottleneck_mining from framing bias (presenting 64-92% BW as uniformly healthy), confidence 80%; (2) WASTED_EFFORT in debate from cold-production gap (0.112x translation factor), confidence 75%.

citation.claim: DAG identified 1 trajectory-changing decision and 2 actionable fixes
citation.quoted_content:
- **Trajectory-changing decisions**: 1
- **Actionable fixes**: 2
